source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The block diagrams of two half-wave rectifiers are shown in the <image>. The transfer characteristics of the rectifiers are also shown within the block.
It is desired to make a full-wave rectifier using the above two half-wave rectifiers. The resultant circuit will be
extraction_notes: image field contains an in-memory PIL image object representation, not a path or URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the block diagrams and transfer characteristics are shown in the image
solving requires identifying visual circuit/diagram structure rather than using text alone
multi_step: the task asks to construct a full-wave rectifier from two given half-wave rectifiers, which implies combining information from both blocks
the prompt references both circuit diagrams and transfer characteristics, suggesting joint visual interpretation plus circuit reasoning
final response is selected from 4 diagram options, so the solver likely must infer the resultant circuit and then map it to a choice
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific option label: 'Diagram B'
choices are explicitly enumerated as A/B/C/D, making correctness easy to check
the expected output appears to be a single discrete choice rather than an open-ended explanation

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantic assets are available
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scoring is strong on image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
the answer is a discrete short-text option, which supports reliable checking
